data.name: Shreyash
data.niche: Agentic AI Engineering, automation (Playwright, Zapier, N8n), Gen AI (Vector DBs, Langchain, OpenClaw)
data.positioning: Final-year CS BTech student & Agentic AI Engineer at Second Brain Labs — self-taught, interviewed 400+ AI engineers across MNCs, built real automations for startups, and ships tools like miniclaw on top of openclaw.
data.audience_description: Tech professionals and students, 20s to 40s, trying to keep up with AI trends. Worried about job loss or being left behind by AI. Not looking for guidance — want real experiences and what actually works.
data.audience_fear: Job loss, irrelevance, AI replacing them, getting lost in the hype with no clear direction
data.audience_aspiration: Stay relevant, understand what's actually happening in AI, learn from someone who's in the trenches doing it
data.current_project: Building a confidential project with OpenClaw for Second Brain Labs, deployed on AWS EC2. Details not public.
data.origin_story: Started in high school preparing for NEET (pre-medical). During COVID in 12th grade, decided to ditch medicine and start learning to code — driven by a childhood interest in tech. Couldn't get BTech CSE admission due to biology/medical background, so took a diploma in CS instead. Since then: built and shipped projects, pitched prototypes to VCs, participated in hackathons, freelanced, and kept building.
specific_milestones: Pitched Tokeno-Phone (blockchain-first decentralised smartphone) to 100x VC at age 18
Participated in MIT iQuHack — MIT's quantum computing hackathon
Built Memory Mo — Android journal app with memory/context, STT and TTS, Gen AI-first
Automated social media posting pipeline using N8n
hot_takes: Execution is the key — ideas mean nothing without shipping
Stop building AI wrappers. Learn the actual underlying tech or you're building on sand.
Most people hyping AI tools haven't actually deployed anything real
data.voice_style: Direct and blunt. Human, conversational tone. Uses '...' for natural pauses. Hates bias in content — presents experience, not agenda.

style_rules.bias: never — present experiences not conclusions for others

posting_examples: Example (old style — do NOT mimic, reference only for vibe): '#LearningInPublic — This year would be fun! Challenged myself to play around Neural Networks. Short term goal: implement Attention is all you need. Will be sharing insights along the journey :>'
data.tagging_strategy: Tag relevant founders, research paper authors, innovators, or companies in posts to boost reach
private_topics: NEET pre-medical exam preparation — never mention in content
